prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for ferrocene were computed using GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding) in gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1 (neutral ground state). The calculation computed Mulliken partial charges for the neutral (N), cation (N−1, multiplicity 2), and anion (N+1, multiplicity 2) states, deriving Fukui indices f⁺ (nucleophilicity), f⁻ (electrophilicity), and the dual descriptor (f⁺ − f⁻) per atom. The iron center was identified as the most reactive site: f⁺(Fe) = 0.22886 and f⁻(Fe) = 0.25938, indicating strong nucleophilic character (negative dual descriptor = −0.030527). All integrity checks passed; charge conservation held for both f⁺ and f⁻ indices (sum ≈ 1.0). No warnings. A visualization bar chart was generated. Software: xtb. Geometry: input XYZ file (ferrocene.xyz, 21 atoms), not pre-optimized.</prose>
<parameter name="value">0.25938191
